title: Free Fire Panel App Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Rebuild the application as a Free Fire game panel app, replacing all hacker/terminal theme content with a Free Fire-themed interface
acceptanceCriteria: App title and branding reference Free Fire
All hacker/matrix/terminal aesthetic is replaced with a Free Fire game UI aesthetic (dark military/battle royale style with orange/red/gold accent colors)
Navigation tabs reflect Free Fire panel sections

file_operations.description: Update the page title from 'Fake Hacker' to 'Free Fire Panel' to reflect the new app branding

Remove the CRT scanline overlay and caffeine.ai attribution footer. Replace the Terminal/Scanner/Profile tab navigation with Free Fire panel sections: Dashboard, Weapons, Characters, and Matches. Remove MatrixRain and ScanlineOverlay components. Update the layout to use a Free Fire-themed structure with a header displaying the app logo/title and main content area for routing

Replace all terminal/hacker theme CSS (terminal-green colors, Share Tech Mono font, glitch animations, CRT scanlines) with Free Fire-themed styling: dark near-black background (#0a0a0a), orange/amber/gold accent colors (e.g., #ff6b35, #f7931e, #fbbf24), military-style typography (use a tactical sans-serif font like Rajdhani or Orbitron), subtle fire glow effects on accent elements using box-shadow and filter properties, and remove blink/glitch/flicker animations

Update the Tailwind theme configuration to replace terminal colors with Free Fire palette: primary accent as orange/amber (--color-ff-primary), dark background as near-black (--color-ff-dark), gold highlights (--color-ff-gold), and tactical red accents (--color-ff-red). Replace monospace fonts with military-style font families. Update box shadows to provide subtle fire glow effects instead of terminal glow. Remove hacker-themed keyframe animations and add new ones if needed for subtle fire or energy effects

requirements.summary: Create a Dashboard/Home page showing Free Fire player stats overview with player nickname, UID, level, rank badge, K/D ratio, win rate, total matches played, and headshot percentage displayed as stylized cards
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays player nickname, UID, and level prominently
Rank badge is displayed with appropriate rank name (e.g., Heroic, Grandmaster)
K/D ratio, win rate, total matches, and headshot % are shown as stat cards
Layout is responsive and styled with Free Fire battle-royale aesthetic

file_operations.description: Create the Dashboard page component displaying a Free Fire player stats overview. Show player nickname prominently at the top with UID below it. Display level as a circular badge or bar. Include a rank badge section showing rank name (e.g., 'Heroic', 'Grandmaster') with an icon or colored tier indicator. Layout stat cards in a responsive grid for K/D ratio, win rate (percentage), total matches played, and headshot percentage. Use static mock data for all stats. Apply Free Fire-themed styling with dark backgrounds, orange/gold accents, and card-based layouts with subtle glow effects on hover

Create a TypeScript module exporting static player stats data: playerNickname (string), playerUID (string), level (number), rankName (string, e.g., 'Heroic'), rankTier (number), kdRatio (number), winRate (number as percentage), totalMatches (number), headshotPercentage (number). Use realistic Free Fire example data

requirements.summary: Create a Weapons page listing popular Free Fire weapons with stats bars for damage, range, fire rate, reload speed, and magazine size, with filtering by weapon type
acceptanceCriteria: Weapons list shows at least 10 popular Free Fire weapons
Each weapon card displays stat bars for key attributes
Filter buttons allow filtering by weapon category
Active filter is visually highlighted

file_operations.description: Create the Weapons page component displaying a list of Free Fire weapons as cards. Include filter buttons at the top for weapon categories: All, AR (Assault Rifle), SMG, Sniper, Shotgun, Pistol. Highlight the active filter with the Free Fire accent color (orange/amber). Each weapon card should display weapon name prominently and include horizontal stat bars for: damage (0-100), range (0-100), fire rate (0-100), reload speed (0-100), and magazine size (0-100). Use static weapon data imported from a separate data module. Layout cards in a responsive grid (2-3 columns on desktop, 1-2 on mobile). Apply Free Fire-themed styling with dark card backgrounds, orange/gold progress bars, and glow effects. Reference the generated weapon category icons at frontend/public/assets/generated/ff-weapon-icons.dim_512x128.png for filter buttons

Create a TypeScript module exporting an array of at least 10 popular Free Fire weapons. Each weapon object should include: id (string), name (string, e.g., 'M1014', 'AK', 'M82B', 'Groza', 'SCAR', 'MP40', 'UMP', 'AWM', 'Kar98k', 'Desert Eagle'), type (enum: 'AR' | 'SMG' | 'Sniper' | 'Shotgun' | 'Pistol'), damage (number 0-100), range (number 0-100), fireRate (number 0-100), reloadSpeed (number 0-100), magazineSize (number 0-100). Use realistic Free Fire weapon stats for authenticity

requirements.summary: Create a Characters page listing Free Fire characters with their special abilities, ability descriptions, and cooldown/duration stats
acceptanceCriteria: At least 8 Free Fire characters are listed
Each card shows character name, ability name, and description
Cards are displayed in a responsive grid
Page uses consistent Free Fire-themed styling

file_operations.description: Create the Characters page component displaying Free Fire characters in a responsive grid (2-3 columns on desktop, 1-2 on mobile). Each character card should show: character name prominently at the top, ability name as a subtitle, ability description (2-3 sentences), and cooldown or duration stats if applicable (e.g., 'Cooldown: 50s', 'Duration: 10s'). Use static character data imported from a separate data module. Apply Free Fire-themed card styling with dark backgrounds, orange/gold borders or accents, and hover glow effects

Create a TypeScript module exporting an array of at least 8 popular Free Fire characters. Each character object should include: id (string), name (string, e.g., 'Alok', 'Chrono', 'Dimitri', 'K', 'Skyler', 'Hayato', 'DJ Alok', 'Moco'), abilityName (string), abilityDescription (string, 2-3 sentences), cooldown (optional string, e.g., '50s'), duration (optional string, e.g., '10s'). Use accurate Free Fire character ability information for authenticity

requirements.summary: Create a Match History page showing a list of simulated recent match entries with map name, placement, kills, damage dealt, survival time, and match mode
acceptanceCriteria: At least 10 recent match entries are shown
Each entry shows placement, kills, damage, survival time, and mode
Booyah (1st place) entries are visually highlighted differently
Entries are listed in reverse chronological order

file_operations.description: Create the Match History page component displaying a list of recent match entries. Each entry should be a card or row showing: map name (e.g., 'Bermuda', 'Purgatory', 'Kalahari'), placement (e.g., '#1 Booyah!', '#5', '#12'), kills (number), damage dealt (number), survival time (e.g., '18:42'), and match mode ('Classic', 'Clash Squad', or 'Ranked'). Visually highlight Booyah (1st place) entries with a gold/orange background or border glow. List entries in reverse chronological order (most recent first). Use static match data imported from a separate data module. Apply Free Fire-themed styling with dark backgrounds and accent colors

Create a TypeScript module exporting an array of at least 10 simulated recent match entries. Each match object should include: id (string), mapName (string, e.g., 'Bermuda', 'Purgatory', 'Kalahari'), placement (number 1-50), kills (number), damageDealt (number), survivalTime (string in MM:SS format), mode (enum: 'Classic' | 'Clash Squad' | 'Ranked'), timestamp (Date or ISO string for sorting). Ensure at least one match has placement = 1 for Booyah highlighting. Order entries from most recent to oldest

requirements.summary: Create a bottom navigation bar with tabs for Home (Dashboard), Weapons, Characters, and Matches, with the active tab visually highlighted
acceptanceCriteria: Bottom nav has four tabs: Home, Weapons, Characters, Matches
Active tab is highlighted with accent color
Navigation switches pages without full reload
Nav bar is fixed at the bottom on mobile viewport

file_operations.description: Replace the existing Terminal/Scanner/Profile tabs with Free Fire panel tabs: Home (links to Dashboard), Weapons, Characters, and Matches. Update the active tab highlighting to use the Free Fire accent color (orange/amber) instead of terminal green. Remove any terminal-themed glow effects and apply Free Fire-themed styling (subtle fire glow on active tab). Ensure the component receives the current active tab via props and highlights it accordingly. The nav bar should remain fixed at the bottom on mobile viewports

Update the routing logic to wire the four Free Fire pages: Dashboard (as Home/default route), Weapons, Characters, and Matches. Pass the current active tab to the BottomNav component. Remove references to Terminal, Scanner, and Profile pages. Ensure navigation switches pages client-side without full reload using React state or a router

requirements.summary: Apply a cohesive Free Fire-themed visual design across the entire app with dark background, orange/amber/gold accent colors, military/tactical typography, subtle fire glow effects, and a header showing the Free Fire Panel logo/title
acceptanceCriteria: App-wide background is dark (e.g., #0a0a0a or similar near-black)
Primary accent color is orange or amber (not blue/purple)
Header displays app branding clearly
Glow or fire effects are applied to accent elements consistently
Font style feels tactical/military while remaining readable

file_operations.description: Create a Header component displaying the Free Fire Panel branding. Include the generated app logo image at frontend/public/assets/generated/ff-panel-logo.dim_256x256.png on the left side, and the app title 'Free Fire Panel' next to it. Optionally include the generated banner image frontend/public/assets/generated/ff-header-banner.dim_1200x300.png as a background or decorative element. Apply Free Fire-themed styling with dark background and orange/gold text and accents. The header should be fixed at the top or positioned at the top of the main layout

Wire the new Header component into the App layout so it appears at the top of every page. Ensure the overall app background is set to dark near-black (#0a0a0a) and that Free Fire accent colors are applied consistently across all page transitions

Ensure the global CSS applies a dark near-black background (#0a0a0a or similar) to the body and root elements. Define CSS custom properties for Free Fire accent colors (e.g., --color-ff-primary: #ff6b35, --color-ff-gold: #fbbf24, --color-ff-red: #dc2626). Import a tactical/military-style font (e.g., Rajdhani, Orbitron, or Saira Condensed) from Google Fonts or use a web-safe fallback. Apply subtle fire glow effects to interactive elements using box-shadow (e.g., 0 0 10px rgba(255, 107, 53, 0.5)) and filter properties. Remove all terminal/hacker-themed styles completely

Extend the Tailwind theme to include Free Fire-specific color tokens (ff-primary, ff-dark, ff-gold, ff-red) mapped to the CSS custom properties defined in index.css. Update fontFamily to use the new military/tactical fonts as defaults. Define utility classes for fire glow effects (e.g., glow-ff) using boxShadow. Remove all terminal-themed color tokens and utility classes
